storage_extensions.usage: mmap values.bin and use values[row] as l_extendedprice in scaled-by-100 revenue arithmetic instead of reading lineitem/l_extendedprice.bin
mmap values.bin and use values[row] as l_discount in scaled-by-100 revenue arithmetic instead of reading lineitem/l_discount.bin

join_order.build: customer(BUILDING membership bitset keyed by c_custkey)
join_order.notes: Resolve the runtime dict code for BUILDING, scan only that posting list, and set a dense customer-membership bitset. Then scan orders in o_orderkey row order; skip blocks with mins >= 9204, fast-accept blocks with maxs < 9204, and test customer membership inline.

join_order.probe: lineitem_orderkey_groups(row-aligned direct lookup by order row)
join_order.notes: For each qualifying order row, use lineitem_orderkey_groups.row_starts[order_row] and row_starts[order_row + 1] (or total lineitem rows for the final group) to derive the contiguous lineitem run, then aggregate qualifying revenue from that run.

data_structures.fact_join: pre_built_index(lineitem_orderkey_groups.row_starts with adjacent-start delta to derive row_count)
data_structures.aggregation: array_direct(thread-local topk heaps; no global group hash table)

indexes.purpose: enumerate BUILDING customer rows without scanning the full customer table
skip or fast-accept order blocks before any row-level o_orderdate load
jump directly from qualifying order row to its contiguous lineitem run

optimization_notes: Root cause: after iteration 1 fixed the join order, the remaining gap is main-scan bandwidth. The plan still streams wide order payload columns and wide lineitem revenue columns, plus a full row_counts array, even though only about 1.48M orders survive the customer and orderdate predicates, only about 6M lineitems are visited, and only the top-k survivors need full order payload. Strategy category E: keep the successful sequential orders-to-lineitem_groups plan, but switch revenue arithmetic to the existing narrow lineitem column versions, derive each lineitem run length from adjacent row_starts instead of scanning row_counts, and late-materialize o_orderdate, o_orderkey, and o_shippriority only for positive-revenue rows that may enter the thread-local top-k. This preserves the cache-resident customer bitset and zone-map pruning while materially reducing main-scan bytes.
